requirements.text: Cyclomatic complexity MUST be ≤10 per function
Each module MUST have a single clearly defined responsibility
Package dependency cycles MUST be 0
Functions MUST have ≤5 parameters; use an options object for more
Functions MUST limit outward calls to ≤7 distinct callees to reduce coupling
Inheritance depth MUST NOT exceed 4 levels
Inheritance hierarchies MUST maintain consistent destructor/cleanup patterns
Components MUST call peers or immediate dependencies, not skip layers
Trust boundaries MUST be enforced between components of different privilege levels
Class hierarchies MUST avoid excessive depth, circular dependencies, and improper virtual destructor patterns
Functions MUST NOT have variadic parameters without type safety or excessive file/data access operations
Code MUST maintain proper encapsulation and data element visibility
Modules MUST NOT have hard-coded network configuration or platform-dependent components
Shared logic MUST be extracted into utility modules rather than duplicated
Public functions MUST NOT depend on global mutable state
Interfaces MUST be preferred over concrete types in function signatures
Configuration MUST be injectable; modules MUST NOT read env vars directly
Serialization control elements MUST be properly implemented
Singleton classes MUST use proper locking and synchronization for instance creation
Data elements containing pointers MUST implement proper copy control
Source files MUST NOT exceed 300 lines
Functions MUST NOT exceed 50 lines
Nesting depth MUST NOT exceed 4 levels
Boolean expressions MUST NOT combine more than 3 conditions without extraction
Public APIs MUST be documented with parameter and return descriptions
Control flow MUST NOT use unconditional jumps out of deeply nested blocks
Loop counter variables MUST NOT be modified inside the loop body
Code complexity MUST remain within measurable thresholds (Halstead, nesting depth, attack surface)
Source code MUST follow consistent naming, formatting, and style conventions
Code MUST NOT contain dead code, empty blocks, irrelevant code, or redundant expressions
Documentation MUST be sufficient, accurate, and consistent with the implementation
Variables MUST be used for their declared purpose with appropriate scope
Comments MUST follow appropriate style and MUST NOT contain suspicious markers
Magic numbers and strings MUST be replaced with named constants
Feature flags MUST be used for in-progress features rather than commented-out code
Wildcard imports (import *) MUST NOT be used
Dead code MUST be removed, not commented out
Commented-out code MUST be removed; use version control for history
Code MUST NOT use obsolete, dangerous, or prohibited functions
Hard-coded literals and magic numbers MUST be replaced with symbolic constants
Global variables MUST NOT be used excessively; shared state MUST be minimized
Data representations MUST NOT be excessively complex or machine-dependent
Test coverage MUST be ≥80% for business-critical modules
Functions with side effects MUST accept dependencies as parameters for test injection
All public functions MUST have corresponding test files
Test files MUST be co-located or mirror the source directory structure
Each test MUST verify a single behaviour with a descriptive name
Input validation frameworks MUST be properly used
Compilation MUST enable sufficient warnings and errors
Code MUST NOT rely on self-modifying techniques
Test setup and teardown MUST clean up all side effects including database state and file system changes
